title: Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses
description: Non-defining clauses add extra info between commas; defining clauses identify without commas.
summary_pt_br: Restritiva (sem vírgula, essencial) vs não-restritiva (com vírgula, informação extra). THAT só na restritiva.

scenes.subtitle_en: Non-defining clauses add extra info between commas; defining clauses identify without commas.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Restritiva (sem vírgula, essencial) vs não-restritiva (com vírgula, informação extra). THAT só na restritiva.

scenes.text_en: Non-defining clauses add extra info between commas; defining clauses identify without commas. Today's focus: defining vs non-defining relative clauses. My sister, who lives in Lisbon, is visiting us next week.
scenes.text_pt: Restritiva (sem vírgula, essencial) vs não-restritiva (com vírgula, informação extra). THAT só na restritiva. Foco de hoje: defining vs non-defining relative clauses. Minha irmã, que mora em Lisboa, vem visitar.

scenes.title: Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses
scenes.body_en: DEFINING (no commas, essential): 'The student WHO won is Brazilian.' NON-DEFINING (commas, extra info): 'Maria, WHO won the prize, is Brazilian.' NEVER use THAT in non-defining clauses.
scenes.body_pt: Restritiva (sem vírgula, essencial) vs não-restritiva (com vírgula, informação extra). THAT só na restritiva.

examples.en: My sister, who lives in Lisbon, is visiting us next week.
examples.pt: Minha irmã, que mora em Lisboa, vem visitar.

examples.en: The book that I am reading is about philosophy.
examples.pt: O livro que estou lendo é sobre filosofia.

examples.en: Brasília, which was built in the sixties, is a planned city.
examples.pt: Brasília, que foi construída nos anos 60, é cidade planejada.

scenes.title: Key vocabulary — Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses

exercise.question: Complete: "My sister, who lives in Lisbon, is ___ us next week."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "My sister, who lives in Lisbon, is visiting us next week."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Minha irmã, que mora em Lisboa, vem visitar.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
The book that I am reading is about philosophy.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "The book that I am reading is about philosophy."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O livro que estou lendo é sobre filosofia.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre defining vs non-defining relative clauses. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Let's review two sentences. Tell me which needs commas.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: My brother who lives abroad is coming home.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Do you have more than one brother?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: No, only one brother who I love dearly.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Then it's non-defining — use commas around that clause.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The book that I read last week was amazing.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You have many books. Which one specifically?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The one that I borrowed from you last week.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Then it's defining — no commas, THAT is fine.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: My mother, who bakes bread, is visiting Sunday.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Perfect non-defining clause. Extra info, commas, no THAT.

scenes.title: A grammar workshop
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write two rules from Kumar's lesson and give one example of each. Use both types of relative clauses.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Kumar spread the student essays on his desk and began marking them with a calm red pen.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: The clause that tells you WHICH person is essential — no commas around it.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: So 'my friend who lives in Rio' is defining because I have many friends?

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Exactly. But 'my mother, who loves music' is non-defining — everyone has one mother.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And we never use THAT in non-defining clauses, right?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Small rules, used daily, separate writers who communicate from writers who impress.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses". Non-defining clauses add extra info between commas; defining clauses identify without commas. My sister, who lives in Lisbon, is visiting us next week. The book that I am reading is about philosophy. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.explanation: She says: "Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Defining vs Non-Defining Relative Clauses".
